{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01Nar7sqXZjRspVwrgjyX5cp","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01AJYT3x5kXcneLMsF3ymo1m","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 - <<'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, glob, numpy as np\n\ndef load(pat, usecols=None):\n    return pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f, usecols=usecols) for f in sorted(glob.glob(pat))], ignore_index=True)\n\n# store-day sales\nsales = load('sales_transaction_*.csv', usecols=['store_id','sales_date','units_sold','revenue','promo_price'])\nsd = sales.groupby(['store_id','sales_date']).agg(\n    units=('units_sold','sum'), revenue=('revenue','sum'),\n    promo_share=('promo_price', lambda s: (s>0).mean())).reset_index()\n\ntraffic = load('store_traffic_*.csv', usecols=['store_id','traffic_date','customer_count'])\nsd = sd.merge(traffic.rename(columns={'traffic_date':'sales_date'}), on=['store_id','sales_date'], how='inner')\nsd['date'] = pd.to_datetime(sd['sales_date'])\n\n# attribute each store-day to last completed visit's rep\nvisits = load('store_visit_*.csv')\nv = visits[visits.completed_visit].copy()\nv['vdate'] = pd.to_datetime(v.visit_start).dt.normalize()\nv = v.sort_values(['store_id','vdate'])\nv = v.drop_duplicates(['store_id','vdate'], keep='last')[['store_id','vdate','rep_id']]\n\nsd = sd.sort_values(['store_id','date'])\nout = []\nfor store, g in sd.groupby('store_id', sort=False):\n    vg = v[v.store_id==store]\n    m = pd.merge_asof(g, vg[['vdate','rep_id']], left_on='date', right_on='vdate', direction='backward')\n    out.append(m)\nsd = pd.concat(out, ignore_index=True).dropna(subset=['rep_id'])\nsd['rep_id'] = sd.rep_id.astype(int)\n\n# outcome: log units per customer, demeaned within store and within month\nsd['y'] = np.log(sd.units.clip(lower=1)) - np.log(sd.customer_count.clip(lower=1))\nsd['month'] = sd.date.dt.month\nsd['y'] = sd.y - sd.groupby('store_id').y.transform('mean')\nsd['y'] = sd.y - sd.groupby('month').y.transform('mean')\n# control promo\nsd['y'] = sd.y - sd.promo_share * np.polyfit(sd.promo_share, sd.y, 1)[0]\n\nr = sd.groupby('rep_id').y.agg(['mean','std','count'])\nr['t'] = r['mean'] / (r['std']/np.sqrt(r['count']))\nr = r.sort_values('t')\nprint(r.head(20).round(3))\nprint(\"...\")\nprint(r.tail(5).round(3))\nmu, sigma = r['mean'].mean(), r['mean'].std()\nr['z'] = (r['mean']-mu)/sigma\nprint(\"\\nreps with z < -2:\", sorted(r[r.z<-2].index.tolist()))\nprint(\"reps with t < -3:\", sorted(r[r.t<-3].index.tolist()))\nr.to_csv('/tmp/rep_sales_effect.csv')\nEOF","description":"Estimate per-rep sales effect via last-visit attribution"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1829,"cache_read_input_tokens":40161,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1829,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":27,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c0fbaa56-2456-4d4e-bfae-faf9b26d913e","uuid":"367fed66-f779-4c02-b0a9-5b5a747b6df3","request_id":"req_011CbwPWc8Rs9D3SMwSutX2K"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01MybavJR12GebDVKRF6JC5Y","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"## Answer\n\n**Underperforming reps: 3, 8, 17, 31, 45, 49, 64, 68, 75, 98** — written to `/app/answer.json`.\n\n## How I identified them\n\n**Data structure.** There are 20 regions, each with 5 reps and 25 stores, and every rep visits every store in their region. Since stores are shared by all 5 regional reps, raw store sales can't be assigned to a single rep — but this structure allows perfectly controlled *within-store* comparisons of the 5 reps serving the same store.\n\n**Step 1 — Execution quality (the rep-controllable factors).** I joined ~487k shelf-audit records to the visiting rep and compared reps within each store (demeaning every metric by store, which removes all store/region/traffic confounders). Ten reps formed a distinct cluster, sitting 1.6–2.2 standard deviations below the field on a composite of facings, eye-level placement, endcap/checkout displays, promo stands, POS materials, and planogram compliance — with a sharp gap to the 11th-worst rep (−0.73). The same reps also had the lowest visit-completion rates. Notably, each of the 10 sits in a different region.\n\n**Step 2 — Confirmation through sales results.** Since a rep's shelf work persists until the next visit touches that product, I attributed each store-product-day's sales to the rep whose shelf audit was most recently in effect for that product, using units sold per store customer (controlling for traffic), demeaned within store-product and month to strip out store size/type/location, product mix, seasonality, and promotions. The 10 flagged reps took ranks 1–8, 12 and 13 of 100 on this sales-impact ranking, all with negative effects (t-statistics down to −3.3) — a concentration that can't occur by chance and confirms their poor in-store execution translates into measurably lower sales."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":552,"cache_read_input_tokens":52408,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":552,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c0fbaa56-2456-4d4e-bfae-faf9b26d913e","uuid":"969668b5-aef4-4f3e-b511-99322d0beef2","request_id":"req_011CbwPguNnaodWYc9AUWxpv"}
